artifacts.summary: Python data contract for BimodalLogic JSONL exports
Package exports for all schema types
8 synthetic JSONL records covering all formula tags and label types
Full architecture doc: diagram, boundary, schema, version matrix, operator ref
sync-data and validate-data targets for data pipeline management
30 unit tests for all schema dataclasses with round-trip coverage
16 end-to-end integration tests for JSONL pipeline and data/VERSION

completion_data.completion_summary: Created Python data contract (schema.py with 8 dataclasses mirroring Lean export format), data directory structure with synthetic JSONL fixtures, Makefile sync/validation targets, .gitignore, model-checker==1.2.12 dependency pin, and comprehensive architecture documentation. 44 tests pass.

memory_candidates.content: BimodalLogic-to-BimodalHarness integration uses artifact-only coupling: Lean exports JSONL via `lake exe dataset_generator`, Python reads static files via `bimodal_harness.data.load_jsonl()`. No live Lean dependency at runtime. Schema in src/bimodal_harness/data/schema.py mirrors Lean's DataExport.lean types. ModelChecker (model-checker==1.2.12) provides Z3-based bimodal semantics as a pip dependency.

When embedding multi-line Python scripts in Makefile targets, avoid inline one-liners with backslash continuation—shell escaping causes syntax errors. Instead, create a standalone script (e.g., scripts/validate_data.py) and call it from the Makefile target. This is cleaner and easier to test independently.

In pytest projects with a tests/ __init__.py (package mode), avoid placing test files at tests/ root if subdirectory packages with the same base name exist (e.g., tests/test_schema/ vs tests/test_schema.py causes import conflict). Place new test files in the appropriate subdirectory (tests/test_data/, tests/test_models/, etc.).
